attempts.code_tried:
theorem module_finite_HModule'_of_isAffineHModuleVanishing
    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
    [IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C F]
    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
    (hU : AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen U) (i : ℕ) (hi : 0 < i) :
    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule' k F i U) :=
  have : Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F i U) :=
    IsAffineHModuleVanishing.subsingleton_HModule' (F := F) hU i hi
  inferInstance
attempts.goal_before: Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule' k F i U) under hypotheses [IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C F], hU, hi
attempts.goal_after: no goals (Mathlib auto-derives Module.Finite R M from Subsingleton M)
attempts.insight: Two-line body (`have ... inferInstance`) is the cleanest possible consumer. Works only because Mathlib auto-derives `Module.Finite` from `Subsingleton`; if that instance were ever weakened the body would need switching to a manual `⟨⟨∅, by simp⟩⟩` construction. Pattern reusable for any 'carrier predicate ⇒ Module.Finite' wrapper for a `Subsingleton`-flavoured class field.
